answer: ValidationError is used when input fails structural or format validation (e.g., a required field is missing, a value is out of allowed range) — it maps to HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity. InvalidDataError is used when the data is structurally valid but semantically incorrect or conflicts with business rules (e.g., a referenced entity does not exist, a value violates a domain constraint). ValidationError belongs closer to the boundary/input parsing layer, while InvalidDataError belongs in the domain or application layer when business rules are violated.
